drug_classes.label: Anticoagulants / antiplatelets
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines that thin the blood or reduce clotting.

drug_classes.label: Antidepressants (SSRIs / SNRIs / MAOIs)
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines for depression and anxiety that raise serotonin.

drug_classes.label: Cardiac glycosides
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines that strengthen and steady the heartbeat (narrow safety margin).

drug_classes.label: Hormonal therapies
drug_classes.consumer_description: Hormone medicines such as the contraceptive pill and hormone replacement.

drug_classes.label: Immunosuppressants
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines that dampen the immune system (e.g. after a transplant).

drug_classes.label: Medicines processed by CYP3A4
drug_classes.consumer_description: A large group of medicines broken down by a common liver enzyme; herbs that speed up or slow this enzyme can change their levels.

drug_classes.label: Sedatives / CNS depressants
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines that calm the nervous system or aid sleep.

drug_classes.label: Thyroid medicines
drug_classes.consumer_description: Medicines that replace or regulate thyroid hormone.

interactions.mechanism: CYP induction increases warfarin clearance and can lower INR, reducing the anticoagulant effect; close monitoring is needed.
St John's wort raises serotonin activity; combined with SSRIs, SNRIs or MAOIs it can trigger serotonin syndrome (agitation, tremor, sweating, rapid heartbeat).
Potent CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein induction lowers antiretroviral levels (indinavir exposure fell ~57%), risking loss of viral control and drug resistance.
P-glycoprotein induction lowers digoxin levels (AUC fell ~25% over ten days), which may reduce its effect.
Increased metabolism of ethinylestradiol and progestins reduces contraceptive exposure, causing breakthrough bleeding, ovulation and unplanned pregnancy.
Enzyme and transporter induction reduces ciclosporin and tacrolimus levels; reported to cause subtherapeutic concentrations and transplant rejection.
As a broad CYP3A4 and P-glycoprotein inducer, St John's wort can lower levels of many medicines cleared by this pathway; check each medication individually.
CYP3A4 induction lowers levels of simvastatin and atorvastatin, potentially weakening their cholesterol-lowering effect.
